root_text.sn44.5:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ca sāriputto, āyasmā ca mahākoṭṭhiko bārāṇasiyaṁ viharanti isipatane migadāye …pe…
root_text.sn44.5:1.3: “Ko nu kho, āvuso, hetu ko paccayo, yenetaṁ abyākataṁ bhagavatā”ti?
root_text.sn44.5:1.4: “Rūpe kho, āvuso, avigatarāgassa avigatacchandassa avigatapemassa avigatapipāsassa avigatapariḷāhassa avigatataṇhassa ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti …pe…
root_text.sn44.5:1.5: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti.
root_text.sn44.5:1.9: viññāṇe avigatarāgassa avigatacchandassa avigatapemassa avigatapipāsassa avigatapariḷāhassa avigatataṇhassa ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti …pe…
root_text.sn44.5:1.10: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa hoti.
root_text.sn44.5:1.15: viññāṇe vigatarāgassa vigatacchandassa vigatapemassa vigatapipāsassa vigatapariḷāhassa vigatataṇhassa ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa na hoti …pe…
root_text.sn44.5:1.16: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipissa na hoti.
root_text.sn44.5:1.17: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo, yenetaṁ abyākataṁ bhagavatā”ti.

translation_text.sn44.5:1.1: At one time Venerable Sāriputta and Venerable Mahākoṭṭhita were staying near Varanasi, in the deer park at Isipatana. …
translation_text.sn44.5:1.3: “What’s the cause, reverend, what’s the reason why this has not been declared by the Buddha?”
translation_text.sn44.5:1.4: “Reverend, if you’re not rid of greed, desire, fondness, thirst, passion, and craving for form …
translation_text.sn44.5:1.9: consciousness, you think ‘a realized one still exists after death’ …
translation_text.sn44.5:1.10: ‘a realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death.’
translation_text.sn44.5:1.11: If you are rid of greed for form …
translation_text.sn44.5:1.15: consciousness, you don’t think ‘a realized one still exists after death’ …
translation_text.sn44.5:1.16: ‘a realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death.’
translation_text.sn44.5:1.17: This is the cause, this is the reason why this has not been declared by the Buddha.”
